category_subtitle: Learn the basic Japanese counter for general things
category_detail:
Counting Things with 「つ」

In Japanese, the counter 「つ」 is used to count general things when you do not know the specific counter or when speaking in a simple, natural way.

This is one of the most important beginner counters because it can be used for many everyday objects.

Important Rule
The 「つ」 counter is mainly used only from 1 to 10.

After 10, Japanese usually does not continue using the same 「つ」 pattern. Instead, speakers often use a more specific counter such as 個 (ko) depending on the object being counted.

1つ (ひとつ / hitotsu)
2つ (ふたつ / futatsu)
3つ (みっつ / mittsu)
10 (とお / too)

Examples after 10
11個 (じゅういっこ / juuikko)
12個 (じゅうにこ / juuniko)

So this category focuses on the standard and natural 「つ」 forms from 1 to 10.

item_list.description: Used to count one general object when the specific counter is unknown.
Used to count two general objects.
Used to count three general objects.
Used to count four general objects.
Used to count five general objects.
Used to count six general objects.
Used to count seven general objects.
Used to count eight general objects.
Used to count nine general objects.
Ten things is read as とお and is the last standard form in the 「つ」 counting pattern.
